note: Crafts of The Open Reach/The Kept Reach (space_time axis). SPANWORK is the space-pole craft of the Horizon-Walkers — nomads for whom everywhere is a place not yet arrived; HOURCRAFT is the time-pole craft of the Hourkeepers, who tend duration and pay for it. The Valley's Shortfold and Waygate are folk-shadows of Spanwork. NOTE (engine): these crafts touch the world-clock (SNG-041) — hour-craft effects are LOCAL to the active frame and never rewrite the shared absolute clock. Authored by Aevi, SNG-054.

abilities.name: Spanwork
abilities.narrationHints: The distance was there a moment ago. It is somehow no longer the point.
abilities.description: The space-pole mastery: distance as a material to be worked. To a Horizon-Walker, 'far' is a claim, and claims can be argued with.
abilities.notFor: Time (Hourcraft's country); arriving somewhere you cannot picture.

tree.name: The Long Stride
tree.grants: Cover ground far past what a body should — a day's march in an hour, tirelessly.
tree.cannot: The ground is still crossed. What is on it still meets you.

tree.name: The Closed Distance
tree.grants: Close a distance you can see — arrive at the far ridge, the far bank, the far door, without crossing the between.
tree.cannot: You must SEE it. And whatever was in the between is still in the between, un-dealt-with.

tree.name: The Open Reach
tree.grants: Distance stops being an obstacle for you and those you carry — the far place is simply a place you may be.
tree.cannot: The world is very large. Horizon-Walkers at this depth stop being FROM anywhere, and it is not always a gift.

abilities.name: Way-Sense
abilities.narrationHints: He knows which way, and does not know how he knows.
abilities.description: Know where you are, where things lie, and the true way between — the first gift of the Open Reach.
abilities.notFor: Reading time; knowing what waits at the far end.

tree.name: Way-Sense
tree.grants: Never be lost. Know your bearing, your distance, and the shape of the way ahead.
tree.cannot: Knowing the way is not knowing what is on it.

tree.name: The Read Road
tree.grants: Read a route's true cost before walking it — where it will be hard, where it will be watched, where it lies.
tree.cannot: A read road is still a road. It does not walk itself.

tree.name: The Whole Map
tree.grants: Hold a region entire in your sense of it — every way in, every way out, every gap between the ways.
tree.cannot: Regions change. The map you hold is the one you built, and it ages.

abilities.name: Hourcraft
abilities.narrationHints: The moment does not pass. It sits there, being looked at.
abilities.description: The time-pole mastery: duration tended, held, and read. The Hourkeepers remember forward and backward, and pay for both.
abilities.notFor: Space (Spanwork's country); undoing what happened — nothing undoes.

tree.name: The Kept Hour
tree.grants: Hold a moment exactly — perceive it whole, at length, while it is still happening.
tree.cannot: The moment still passes for everyone else. You get to look; you do not get to act longer.

tree.name: The Slow Hour
tree.grants: Slow duration around you — a fight, a fall, a conversation stretches, and you have room inside it.
tree.cannot: It costs continuously and it costs steeply. And the world outside your slow hour arrives on time regardless.

tree.name: The Pre-Remembered
tree.grants: Remember forward — an hour that has not happened arrives in you as memory does, with a memory's specificity and a memory's unreliability.
tree.cannot: You cannot tell a true pre-memory from a feared one. Hourkeepers who trust it wrongly are the reason the craft is feared.

abilities.name: Hour-Sense
abilities.narrationHints: She knows how long it has been, and how long it has left.
abilities.description: Know duration truly — how long since, how long until — the first gift of the Kept Reach.
abilities.notFor: Knowing WHAT will happen. This knows WHEN.

tree.name: Hour-Sense
tree.grants: Know exactly how long since a thing happened, and how long until a thing due will come.
tree.cannot: When, never what. The hour is knowable; the event is not.

tree.name: The Long Count
tree.grants: Read the age of a place, a wound, a working, or a lie — and when it will fail.
tree.cannot: It counts. It does not care whether you are ready.

tree.name: The Kept Ledger
tree.grants: Hold the whole duration of a place or a people — its beginning, its hours, and the hour it is running out of.
tree.cannot: Knowing a people's remaining hours and being unable to say so is the Hourkeepers' particular grief.

abilities.name: The Long Reach
abilities.narrationHints: The distance was their advantage. He closed it in the throwing.
abilities.description: Attack at range or in motion — the horizon fighter's harm is always the far brought suddenly close.

tree.name: Distance Closed
tree.grants: Strike from further away than expected — a thrown weapon, a sprint into contact, an attack timed at the moment of arrival.

tree.name: The Running Strike
tree.grants: Attack in full motion — without losing accuracy or force to the movement; the speed is the weapon.

tree.name: From Wherever You Are
tree.grants: Distance ceases to reduce your effectiveness — whether across a room or across a field, you arrive with full force or make the throw as if there were no gap at all.

abilities.name: The Fresh Horizon
abilities.narrationHints: The next ridge. The next valley. The sight of more ahead is what keeps them going.
abilities.description: Distance restores — a journey that heals, the sight of the next horizon renewing the will to go on.

tree.name: New Ground
tree.grants: Move to a new place and the weight of the last one lifts — wounds feel less, fear recedes, the body and will recover in the way only distance makes possible.

tree.name: The Road Restores
tree.grants: A journey restores the party — physically and otherwise; the movement is the medicine, and you know how to pace it so it heals rather than depletes.

tree.name: Always More Ahead
tree.grants: Show someone the next horizon and it fills them again — despair fails against the sight of further to go, and you can make that sight real.

abilities.name: The Kept Distance
abilities.narrationHints: The threat was real. She simply stayed where it wasn't.
abilities.description: Protection through range — keep the threat far and its power collapses.

tree.name: Out of Reach
tree.grants: Maintain enough distance that what threatens you cannot effectively reach you — moving before it closes, keeping the gap that keeps you safe.

tree.name: The Managed Gap
tree.grants: Control the distance between yourself and a threat — always too far for it to act, close enough for you to — and maintain that gap through a whole engagement.

tree.name: Untouchable Distance
tree.grants: Stay perpetually where the threat isn't — and bring others with you. The person who cannot close the gap cannot win.

abilities.name: The Made Crossing
abilities.narrationHints: A gap is a statement about what the land thinks is possible. He disagreed.
abilities.description: Create a crossing where there was none — not by building but by knowing where the distance can be made to yield.

tree.name: The Found Way
tree.grants: Find a crossing across an obstacle that wasn't apparent — the shallows, the ledge, the fallen tree — by reading terrain the way a horizon-walker does.

tree.name: Where the Gap Yields
tree.grants: Identify and exploit the specific point where an obstacle can be crossed — not the obvious attempt but the place where the distance relents.

tree.name: No Uncrossable Gap
tree.grants: Make the crossing — any crossing — by knowing exactly where to commit and exactly how. What the land calls uncrossable is only waiting for someone who can read its grain.

abilities.name: The Land Knowledge
abilities.narrationHints: She knew where the pass was before anyone asked. That was enough.
abilities.description: Command through knowing what others don't — the terrain, the route, the distances that change everything about what's possible.

tree.name: The Scout's Word
tree.grants: Speak with the authority of someone who has been where others haven't — your intelligence about terrain, routes, and distances commands genuine trust.

tree.name: The Strategic Ground
tree.grants: Use knowledge of land and distance to direct others effectively — the advice that wins a chase, the route that avoids the trap, the position that changes everything.

tree.name: Known Land
tree.grants: In territory you have walked, your knowledge is comprehensive enough to command any situation that depends on terrain — you are the map and the map is right.

abilities.name: The Road Ahead
abilities.narrationHints: As long as there is further to go, they go.
abilities.description: Sustain through forward motion — the horizon-walker who keeps moving doesn't run out.

tree.name: Keep Walking
tree.grants: Sustain movement and function past the ordinary limit — as long as there is ground ahead, you cover it.

tree.name: The Sustained March
tree.grants: Keep a party moving and functional across brutal distances — through exhaustion, injury, and conditions that would stop others — by knowing how to pace the impossible road.

tree.name: No Stopping Place
tree.grants: You and your party can go on for as long as the task requires — rest when you must, move when you must — until the road has been walked.

abilities.name: The Wrong Moment
abilities.narrationHints: She waited for the moment they couldn't answer. Then she acted.
abilities.description: Strike or act at the precise moment when the other person cannot respond — the hourkeeper's harm is perfect timing, not force.

tree.name: Their Bad Moment
tree.grants: Identify and act in the precise instant when a person is off-balance, distracted, or committed — so your action lands without meaningful response.

tree.name: Chosen Time
tree.grants: Dictate when the confrontation happens by waiting for the exact moment that advantages you most — the ambush, the interruption, the word at the one instant it cannot be answered.

tree.name: No Good Moment for Them
tree.grants: Act in a sequence of moments so precisely chosen that the other person is never in a position to respond effectively — they are always recovering from the last thing when the next arrives.

abilities.name: The Given Time
abilities.narrationHints: She gave them the hour they needed that wasn't there.
abilities.description: Stretch a moment or buy time that wasn't available — so that what needed longer to heal or resolve has it.

tree.name: A Little More Time
tree.grants: Buy the minutes or hours that make a difference — slow enough that care can happen, or hold the situation open long enough that recovery begins.

tree.name: The Hour Given
tree.grants: Create the time a person or situation needs — not by stopping anything, but by managing the duration so that what needs time to work has time to work.

tree.name: All the Time It Needs
tree.grants: Hold open the duration of a recovery, a negotiation, or a healing — as long as it requires — without the outside closing in until it is ready.

abilities.name: The Wasted Moment
abilities.narrationHints: She knew the blow was coming. She was elsewhere in it.
abilities.description: Protect through perfect foreknowledge of timing — make their moment of action count for nothing by not being in it.

tree.name: Their Moment Wasted
tree.grants: See the attack forming early enough to be gone before it lands — not blocking, not armoring, but the exact move that empties the moment of consequence.

tree.name: No Good Window
tree.grants: Protect someone by watching their threat's timing and calling the move — the dodge, the step, the word — at the instant that makes the attack miss without effort.

tree.name: The Protected Duration
tree.grants: For as long as you attend to a person's safety, no moment that could harm them arrives — you see it and fill or empty it before it becomes a wound.

abilities.name: The Shaped Duration
abilities.narrationHints: The hour was not the same hour as before. She had worked on it.
abilities.description: Shape how long something takes — stretch a moment, compress an hour, make duration do what you need rather than what it would naturally do.

tree.name: The Stretched Moment
tree.grants: Make a short span feel and function longer — enough to act fully in what should only be an instant.

tree.name: Compressed Duration
tree.grants: Collapse a long span into less — a day's travel has the fatigue of an hour, a complex process completes in time it shouldn't.

tree.name: Duration to Order
tree.grants: Shape how long something takes with precision — bend the duration of any ongoing process to what you need it to be.

abilities.name: The Opened Moment
abilities.narrationHints: She stepped between the moments and was already through.
abilities.description: Open a gap in the flow of events to move through — the hourkeeper's passage happens between moments others don't see.

tree.name: Between Moments
tree.grants: Move in the gap between one moment and the next — where attention lapses, where the pattern holds its breath — and arrive without being witnessed.

tree.name: The Cleared Interval
tree.grants: Open a window in time — a pause in a guard rotation, a silence in a conversation, a beat between alarms — and move through it cleanly.

tree.name: The Moment Made
tree.grants: Create the gap that didn't exist — find or open the instant that allows passage — and step through it, bringing others if needed.

abilities.name: The Long Watch
abilities.narrationHints: She felt time differently. Waiting was not the hardship it was for others.
abilities.description: Endure through understanding duration — the hourkeeper who feels time differently can wait, hold, or persist longer than anyone else.

tree.name: Patient Duration
tree.grants: Hold a position, a vigil, a watch — past the point where others have lost focus or given in — because the passage of time does not wear on you as it does on them.

tree.name: The Longer Wait
tree.grants: Sustain any activity that requires patience across duration — a negotiation, a stake-out, a long recovery — by feeling the time accurately rather than agonizingly.

tree.name: As Long as It Takes
tree.grants: You can hold as long as the situation requires — the vigil, the wait, the watch — and remain fully functional throughout. Time is not your enemy.
